evidence: turn.change exists to teach listeners a NEW per-turn contextId on the BROWSER path, where the client mints a fresh contextId per turn. It is emitted only by pushTurnChange() in voice-server-websocket/src/index.ts (lines 652/682/695, def at 788), guarded by `if (nextContextId !== currentContextId)`. On telephony the contextId is STABLE per call: twilio.ts:294, telnyx.ts:312, smartpbx.ts:259 all set `state.contextId = contextIdFn(start)` once at the `start` event and emit every `user.audio_received` with that fixed id (twilio.ts:309-310, smartpbx.ts:277-278). So nextContextId never changes and turn.change is correctly never needed. Critically, every reset that turn.change provides on the browser path is ALSO provided by VAD/audio on the telephony path: (1) session.lastFinalizedContextId is reset NOT only by the turn.change handler (voice-agent-session.ts:471-473) but also by handleVadSpeechStarted at line 621 (`this.lastFinalizedContextId = "";`), which fires on EVERY new user utterance via vad.speech_started (emitted transport-agnostically by voice-vad-silero/src/index.ts:232). Since speech_started necessarily precedes eos.turn_complete, the dedup guard at voice-agent-session.ts:677 (`if (this.lastFinalizedContextId === pkt.contextId)`) is correctly cleared per turn on telephony. (2) Deepgram STT's currentContextId is set from every stt.audio packet (voice-stt-deepgram/src/index.ts:165-167), not just turn.change (line 178-182); streamStartTime is also reset by interrupt.stt (line 185-188). (3) Google STT likewise sets currentContextId from every stt.audio packet (voice-stt-google/src/index.ts:117), making its turn.change handler (line 120-122) redundant on the stable-id path. The finding's premise — that stable-contextId telephony calls miss per-turn resets — is wrong: VAD speech_started and stt.audio supply those resets.
real_fix: No fix needed. turn.change is a browser-only mechanism for the new-contextId-per-turn model; on telephony the contextId is intentionally stable and the per-turn state resets are driven by vad.speech_started (session.lastFinalizedContextId at voice-agent-session.ts:621) and by stt.audio (STT currentContextId). Emitting turn.change from telephony would be a no-op (contextId equals itself) at best, or spurious churn if forced.
